chunks.section: Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSOs & Programme Outcomes (POs)
chunks.context: This chunk contains the AIML department Vision, Mission statements, Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs), Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs), and all 12 Programme Outcomes (POs).
chunks.text: Vision of AIML Department: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Engineers using through sustainable practices. Mission of AIML Department: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1. Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3. Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): PSO1. Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies/tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2. Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions. PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet specified needs. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data. PO5 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools. PO6 The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues. PO7 Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts. PO8 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and norms of engineering practice. PO9 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, member or leader in diverse teams and multidisciplinary settings. PO10 Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities, write reports, design documentation, make presentations. PO11 Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge of engineering and management principles as a member and leader. PO12 Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and engage in independent and life-long learning in technological change.

chunks.section: AI & ML Job Roles, Salary Trends & Employment Forecast India 2025-2030
chunks.context: This chunk covers the AI and ML employment forecast for India from 2025-2030, key job roles, required skills, salary ranges, and industry-specific trends.
chunks.text: AI and ML Employment Forecast in India 2025-2030: The AI and ML sectors in India are poised for substantial growth over the next five years. Key Drivers: 1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — AI/ML adopted across healthcare, finance, e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, agriculture. Companies leveraging AI/ML for automation, data analytics, predictive analytics, personalization, customer service. Indian companies like Infosys, Wipro, TCS investing heavily in AI/ML. 2. Government Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, AI for All, AI-enabled Smart Cities, AI-powered education. 3. AI and Automation in Enterprises — Robotic Process Automation (RPA) integrated with AI and NLP creating job opportunities. 4. R&D and Innovation — Google, Microsoft, IBM, Intel have set up R&D centers in India. 5. Increased Investment in Startups — AI startups attracting substantial venture capital investment. 6. Healthcare, Autonomous Vehicles, Smart Cities — Major AI integration areas. Key AI and ML Job Roles (2025-2030): Machine Learning Engineer — Develop and deploy ML models. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, neural networks, deep learning. High demand in e-commerce, finance, automotive, manufacturing. Data Scientist — Analyze and interpret complex data. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Matplotlib, Seaborn, ML algorithms, big data tools. In demand in finance, retail, healthcare, telecom. AI Research Scientist — Conduct cutting-edge research in NLP, computer vision, reinforcement learning. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, mathematical modeling. Deep Learning Engineer — Build neural networks for computer vision, NLP, speech recognition. Skills: TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch. NLP Engineer — Develop AI systems for human language using ML techniques. Skills: SpaCy, NLTK, sentiment analysis, speech recognition. AI Software Engineer — Design and build AI applications. Skills: Python, C++, Java, TensorFlow, cloud computing, Docker, Kubernetes. AI Ethics Specialist — Ensure ethical AI, bias reduction, fairness, transparency. AI/ML Product Manager — Manage AI product development. Skills: Agile, data analysis, market research. Expected Salary Ranges: Entry-level (0-2 years): Rs. 6-12 Lakhs per annum. Mid-level (3-5 years): Rs. 12-20 Lakhs per annum. Senior-level (5+ years): Rs. 20-35 Lakhs per annum. AI Research Scientist: Rs. 25-50 Lakhs per annum. AI/ML Project Managers (Senior): Rs. 30-60 Lakhs per annum. Job Market Growth: CAGR of 30-40% in next 5 years. Total AI jobs could double or triple.

chunks.section: Industry-Specific AI Trends, Certifications & Targeted Job Applications
chunks.context: This chunk covers industry-specific AI and ML job trends across healthcare, finance, automotive, retail sectors, recommended certifications to upskill, and target companies for job applications.
chunks.text: Industry-Specific AI and ML Job Trends: Healthcare — Medical diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Medical AI, Bioinformatics, and healthcare data analytics. Finance and Banking — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Personalized banking services, Automated trading, Financial data scientists, Quantitative analysts, AI-enabled risk management. ML applications in Fintech will create opportunities for specialists in Blockchain, smart contracts, and AI in Cryptocurrency. Automotive (Autonomous Vehicles) — Self-driving cars and Smart transportation systems. AI and ML professionals required for computer vision, sensor fusion, and real-time decision-making for autonomous vehicles. Retail and E-commerce — AI transforming customer experience through personalized recommendations, dynamic pricing. Demand for professionals in e-commerce AI, recommendation algorithms, and supply chain optimization. Certifications and Skills to Upskill: 1. Python and R language for data manipulation and model development. 2. TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras for deep learning. 3. Data science tools: Pandas, NumPy, SciPy for data wrangling and analysis. 4. NLP frameworks: SpaCy, Hugging Face for text processing and sentiment analysis. 5. Google AI Certification and Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng (Coursera). 6. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer. 7. AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. 8. Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate. 9. IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate. 10. Data Science Professional Certificate. 11. Certified Data Scientist (CDS). 12. Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng. 13. Advanced Machine Learning Specialization. 14. Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning. Targeted Job Applications: AI-centric companies — Google, Microsoft, Amazon, NVIDIA, IBM, TCS, and Infosys. Startups — India's growing AI/ML startups in Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune offer hands-on work in cutting-edge AI/ML applications. Freelancing — Platforms like Upwork and Freelancer offer freelance opportunities in AI/ML.

chunks.section: Alumni Association Overview and Objectives
chunks.context: Describes the purpose, mission, and seven core objectives of the MSAJCE Alumni Association, useful for students and alumni wanting to know what the association does.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Alumni Association — Overview Alumni association of Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering welcomes you all to join us. This association is formed to reconnect and foster intellectual and emotional relationship between alumni and parent organisation. Also to bring in goodwill and voluntary commitment to support future engineers of the institution and provide industry-institute relationship and enthusiastically participate in various activities organised by the college. Our Objectives: 1. To foster a sense of connectedness back to the institution 2. To establish and renew student relationship 3. To involve in various activities of the college 4. To provide assistance to needful students by way of funds and scholarship 5. To gather and maintain database of employment and assist in placements 6. To arrange teaching and training classes to students 7. To contribute for the overall upgrading of the institution Alumni Registration Form: https://forms.gle/CBMdXCDEb9uXVEBv6 Association Reports and Documents: ALUMNI ASSOCIATION BY-LAWS (Tamil): https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/alumni/Alumni-Bylaw.pdf ALUMNI MEET MINUTES: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/alumni/543-B-ALUMNIMEETMINUTES.pdf ALUMNI ASSOCIATION BY-LAWS (English): https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/alumni/Alumni-Bylaw-English.pdf ALUMNI MEET REPORT: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/alumni/543-C-ALUMNIMEETREPORT.pdf

chunks.section: Sports Club — Introduction, Objectives, Vision, Motto and Games
chunks.context: Describes the MSAJCE Sports Club — its purpose, objectives, vision, motto, and the list of indoor and outdoor games played, useful for students wanting to know sports facilities and activities.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Sports Club Introduction: This club maintains records of sports and games events attended by students within the college, within the university and outside at the region/state/national level and their achievements/awards. It submits annual reports on sports/events and budget allocations and spending during the year. It conducts sports events that students need and rewards them with trophies and medals. All kinds of sports events take place in this club. Objectives: 1. To build up sportsmanship. 2. To foster intimate friendships. 3. To reduce stress and improve health. 4. To improve skills and enhance employability. 5. To support in developing positive attitude, self-confidence, courage and patience. 6. To get opportunities to see the community closely and thus get an experience of human nature in relation to his/her environment. 7. To avail the opportunity to meet people from different walks of life. Vision: To encourage and motivate students to involve in various sports activities at both inter and intra college level. Motto: "Teamwork Makes The Dream Work" Games Conducted: Outdoor Games: Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, Throughball Indoor Games: Carrom, Table Tennis, Chess

rows: Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, Throughball

chunks.section: Fine Arts Club (ENVISTA CLUB — ARTFUL AESTHETICS) — Introduction, Objectives, Vision, Motto and Student Leaders
chunks.context: Describes the MSAJCE Fine Arts Club under the ENVISTA CLUB umbrella, including its 8 objectives, vision, motto, staff coordinator, and current student president and vice president.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Fine Arts Club — ARTFUL AESTHETICS (Part of ENVISTA CLUB) Introduction: Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering has successfully inaugurated the ENVISTA CLUB. In total, 10 clubs are going to take part actively in this umbrella. Objectives: 1. The main objective of the fine arts club is to encourage the budding talent of the students. 2. Impart skill-based training and multi-disciplinary research activities with industry and reputed institute collaborations. Provide opportunities and encouragement to express oneself freely. 3. Educational objectives of this club are to encourage imagination, creative ability and introduce an artistic environment. 4. Inspiring students to make maximum contributions as creative artists and citizens. 5. Organize various competitions, functions, and events in the college. 6. Make the student confident and competitive in the global environment. 7. To improve the imagination skill in entertainment. 8. To give students an artistic outlet and to help each enrich interest for art and personal experience through art. Staff Coordinator: S. Viswajaa — Assistant Professor, IT Department Motto: The motto of the Fine Arts Club is to inspire and enable students to actively participate and develop their talent and skills like Dance, Art, Singing, Music, Acting etc. Through this club students can develop their skills and talents as well as feel relaxation and enjoy their college life. The club plans to conduct several events for extra-curricular activities. Students are free to participate in this club and expose their talents. The club also makes students participate in external college fests to showcase their talents. Vision: To encourage students to express their thoughts, feelings and creativity through various visual art forms from two dimensions to three dimensions. To tap the inherent talents and potentials of the student community at all levels. Student President: Kishore P — IV Year Mechanical Student Vice President: Laksha — III Year CSE

chunks.section: Science Club — Introduction, Objectives and Activities
chunks.context: Describes the MSAJCE Science Club — its purpose of inviting scientists, three sections (Physics, Chemistry, General), guiding principles, and planned activities including experiments and workshops.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Science Club Introduction: The Science Club was started with an aim to invite eminent scientists who would share their experience with students and motivate them to gain scientific knowledge. Activities are designed primarily to introduce students to undertake interesting and exciting science and technology projects. The Science Club consists of a group of students who want to explore the many branches of engineering, mathematics, and science. The club is open to all students. The purpose of the Science Club is to promote interest, understanding, and knowledge of the scientific world amongst college students and the local community. Science Club is one of the largest clubs in the college. The club is divided into three sections: Physics, Chemistry, and General. The club aims to arouse engineering students' interest in learning science, promoting academic achievement in Science subjects. It also helps students to learn more about science outside the classroom and teaches how to apply scientific knowledge in daily life through activities. The club also guides students to develop an independent, logical and creative mind. Activities include experiments in: making electronic components, making invisible ink, electroplating, biodiesels, fire writing, and making venation of leaves. Information about the science club and day-to-day updates are displayed on the notice board. Objectives of Science Club: The five Guiding Principles govern how activities have been developed. Investigator activity offers students the opportunity to: 1. Solve a relevant, science-based problem set within a scenario. 2. Work in pairs or small groups or independently. 3. Take part in practical, hands-on science activities. 4. Think and talk about science during the activity and when sharing results. 5. Share results using a variety of media. Things to Do in Science Club: Conduct Seminars, Workshops, Exhibitions and Quiz competitions to inculcate knowledge in the field of science. Make students acquire knowledge by giving lectures once a month. Promote the use of IT in learning science. Encourage student participation in activities organized by the Science Club. Help students promote discussion on recent innovations in the field of science once a month. Decorate the walls of the classroom, library and laboratory with scientific pictures and charts prepared by students themselves.

chunks.section: Tamil Mandram — Introduction, Objectives and Events
chunks.context: Describes the MSAJCE Tamil Mandram — a club dedicated to promoting Tamil language, culture and ancient heritage among students, with its objectives and list of cultural events conducted.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Tamil Mandram (Tamil Language and Culture Club) Introduction: The Tamil Mandram was formed to bring about a wonderful transformation in the youth to keep the Tamil language alive and thriving. The motto of the Mandram is: "Thithikkum then tamil mozhi endrum vaazha vendum! Engum vella vendum!" (Translation: "The sweet honey Tamil language must live forever! Must win everywhere!") The Mandram works to revive and reconstruct the ancient Tamil culture and the identities left behind by ancestors. The vision is for the youth to take pride in their language and move from just speaking Tamil to actively bringing glory to Tamil through action. "Vaazha Tamil! Velga Tamil!" (Translation: "Long live Tamil! Victory to Tamil!") Objectives of Tamil Mandram: 1. To develop Tamil enthusiasm and knowledge among students — this is the primary objective of the Tamil Mandram. 2. To spread ancient Tamil culture and heritage among today's youth. 3. To develop technical knowledge in Tamil. 4. To continuously conduct Tamil events and social activities suited to the times. Events Conducted by Tamil Mandram: 1. Irumugam Oru Agam (Two faces, one heart — elocution/drama) 2. Pessum Padam (Speaking Picture — storytelling) 3. Aadu Puli (Tiger and Goat — traditional game) 4. Kaivanna Kaviyam (Colourful Poetry — art and poetry) 5. Tent Kottai (Tent Camp — cultural event) 6. Vidugadhai Vilaiyattu (Riddle Games) 7. Pirasangatin Thalaiwan (King of Speeches — debate/oratory competition) 8. Tamilkkorvai (Tamil Chain — literary event)

rows: Two faces, one heart — elocution/drama event

chunks.context: Describes the MSAJCE Coding Club — its aims to promote coding contests, workshops on web development, ML and IoT, participation in ACM-ICPC and Google events, and weekly club activities.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Coding Club About: Programming abilities are crucial tools that can be applied to and integrated into a variety of disciplines and domains in today's climate of rapid change. Aim: 1. More coding contests should be held to reach all students on campus. 2. Conduct workshops on computer science topics like web development, application development, machine learning, IoT, etc. 3. Participate in open source initiatives, create websites and applications, and offer technical assistance to clubs and groups on campus. 4. Represent the college at intercollegiate competitions. 5. Take part in competitions like ACM-ICPC, Google Code Jam, Google Summer of Code, and other online coding events. Practice: 1. The club holds a college-wide coding competition to find new members who are enthusiastic about coding. The competition emphasizes reasoning, analysis, and problem-solving abilities in addition to coding. 2. The club gets together once a week to talk and work on initiatives. To accomplish a shared goal, all members work together. Workshops and technical discussions on various competitions and computer science topics are conducted through the club. 3. Lecture sessions are also organized for the benefit of members, where some computer science principles are taught. 4. The group also hosts seminars and workshops by seniors and former members on how to prepare for internships and job placement.

chunks.section: Energy and Eco Club — Energy Club and Eco Club
chunks.context: Describes the two components of the MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club — the Energy Club focused on sustainable energy awareness and conservation, and the Eco Club focused on environmental responsibility.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club Energy Club: The goal of the Energy Club is to raise and spread knowledge about responsible and sustainable energy use. Given the limited quantity of fossil fuels available, resource management and conservation are urgently needed. Students are introduced to the concept of energy conservation through the Energy Club, as well as the necessity for it and what part they can play in guaranteeing conservation. In order to combat the ongoing energy issue, it helps the students to conserve energy. These actions would support not only the dissemination of information about energy efficiency but also its application in homes and institutions. Eco Club: The Eco Club holds that nature imparts life lessons in its own steady ways and empowers our young students to share their responsibility for upholding environmental health and taking action to prevent environmental degradation both individually and collectively. The club encourages students to take both individual and collective action to protect the environment.

chunks.text: MSAJCE Photography Club Introduction: The members of the photography club are encouraged to take images as they grow to appreciate the hobby more. Visual arts exploration The club is open to all expertise levels — beginners and advanced photographers are both welcome. chunks.section: CSBS Department Overview, Key Features & Programme Details
chunks.context: This chunk describes the CSBS department overview including what CSBS is, TCS industry collaboration, key features, programme details with seats and eligibility, and benefits over regular CSE.
chunks.text: Department of Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) — Overview: B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is a specialized undergraduate engineering program designed to blend core computer science education with insights into business systems and management. It is a relatively new course introduced in collaboration with industry leaders like Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the dynamic needs of the IT and business world. The programme aims to enhance the relevance of Computer Science to meet future demands of the IT industry in the era of Business 4.0. The curriculum ensures exposure to Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, Internet of Things, Design Thinking, along with humanities, value-added science, and interrelated topics. Key Features: 1. Balanced Curriculum — Core CS subjects (Data Structures, Algorithms, OS, DBMS, AI/ML) + Business and Management subjects (Economics, Financial Management, Business Process Management). 2. Industry Collaboration — Designed in partnership with TCS; emphasis on employability and industry readiness. 3. Focus Areas — Problem-solving using computational thinking, Business analytics and decision-making, IT systems for business applications. 4. Soft Skills Training — Communication, Teamwork, Entrepreneurial thinking. Top Recruiters: TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, CTS. Startups and FinTech companies like FSS. Benefits Over Regular CSE: More interdisciplinary exposure; better suited for roles in tech-enabled business functions; greater alignment with industry expectations in IT services and consulting. Programme Details: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry). Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry). Total Seats: 30. Government Quota: 15. Management Quota: 15. Eligibility: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Curriculum: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

CONTENT: Department of Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) — Overview: B.Tech in Computer Science and Business Systems (CSBS) is a specialized undergraduate engineering program designed to blend core computer science education with insights into business systems and management. It is a relatively new course introduced in collaboration with industry leaders like Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) to bridge the gap between traditional computer science education and the dynamic needs of the IT and business world. The programme aims to enhance the relevance of Computer Science to meet future demands of the IT industry in the era of Business 4.0. The curriculum ensures exposure to Analytics, Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, Business Analytics, Financial Management, Economics, Internet of Things, Design Thinking, along with humanities, value-added science, and interrelated topics. Key Features: 1. Balanced Curriculum — Core CS subjects (Data Structures, Algorithms, OS, DBMS, AI/ML) + Business and Management subjects (Economics, Financial Management, Business Process Management). 2. Industry Collaboration — Designed in partnership with TCS; emphasis on employability and industry readiness. 3. Focus Areas — Problem-solving using computational thinking, Business analytics and decision-making, IT systems for business applications. 4. Soft Skills Training — Communication, Teamwork, Entrepreneurial thinking. Top Recruiters: TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, CTS. Startups and FinTech companies like FSS. Benefits Over Regular CSE: More interdisciplinary exposure; better suited for roles in tech-enabled business functions; greater alignment with industry expectations in IT services and consulting. Programme Details: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry). Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry). Total Seats: 30. Government Quota: 15. Management Quota: 15. Eligibility: 10+2 with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Curriculum: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.
KEYWORDS: CSBS, Computer Science Business Systems, B.Tech CSBS, TCS collaboration, 30 seats, Business Analytics, Cloud Computing, Machine Learning, Financial Management, Economics, Design Thinking, IoT, industry partnership, Business 4.0, interdisciplinary

chunks.section: Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSOs & All 12 Programme Outcomes (POs)
chunks.context: This chunk contains the CSBS department Vision, Mission statements, Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs), Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs), and all 12 Programme Outcomes (POs).
chunks.text: Vision of CSBS Department: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient IT Professionals with Business Skills through sustainable practices. Mission of CSBS Department: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Information Science and Business through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate Business thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1. Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3. Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): PSO1. Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies/tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2. Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and specialization to solve complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems. PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems meeting specified needs. PO4 Conduct Investigations: Use research-based knowledge and methods for valid conclusions. PO5 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering/IT tools. PO6 Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues. PO7 Environment and Sustainability: Understand impact of engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts. PO8 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and norms. PO9 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as individual, member or leader in diverse and multidisciplinary teams. PO10 Communication: Communicate effectively, write reports, design documentation, make presentations. PO11 Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge of engineering and management principles. PO12 Life-long Learning: Recognize need for and engage in independent and life-long learning in technological change.

chunks.section: Employment Growth 2025-2030 — Key Drivers & Digital Transformation
chunks.context: This chunk covers the key employment growth drivers for CSBS professionals in India from 2025-2030 including digital transformation, AI/ML, cloud computing, e-commerce, and FinTech expansion.
chunks.text: Employment Growth in Computer Science and Business Systems 2025-2030 in India: Significant growth is expected driven by increasing digitalization, rise in e-commerce, adoption of AI, and transformation of business processes. India is positioning itself as a hub for IT services and business innovation. Key Drivers: 1. Digital Transformation of Businesses — Business Process Automation using AI, cloud technologies, and big data. Growth of ERP systems like SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics. Shift towards SaaS models requires professionals to implement and integrate solutions. 2. Growth of AI, Machine Learning, and Analytics — Businesses rely on data for decision-making, boosting demand for data analysts, data scientists, and business intelligence professionals. AI and ML used in CRM, predictive analytics, and inventory management. 3. Cloud Computing and Digital Platforms — Cloud migration requires cloud architects, cloud engineers, and business analysts. Multi-cloud strategies using AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud require professionals with both technical and business knowledge. 4. E-Commerce Growth and Customer Experience — India's growing e-commerce market needs professionals for online platforms, AI-powered customer service tools (chatbots, recommendation systems). Supply chain optimization creating demand for data scientists and systems analysts. 5. Enterprise Digitalization — FinTech: Digital payment systems, blockchain, AI-powered financial services. Healthcare: Digital records, telemedicine, AI for diagnostics. Smart Manufacturing: Industry 4.0, IoT, AI in manufacturing bridges gap between technical systems and business operations. 6. Cyber Security and Compliance — Increased cyber threats driving demand for cyber defense, data protection, regulatory compliance roles. Evolving data protection laws (GDPR and India's Personal Data Protection Bill) require risk management and privacy experts.

chunks.section: Key Job Roles in High Demand — CSBS Graduates 2025-2030
chunks.context: This chunk details all key high-demand job roles for CSBS graduates with responsibilities and required skills for each role including Business Systems Analyst, Data Scientist, Cloud Architect, ERP Consultant, AI/ML Engineer, Cyber Security Specialist, BI Analyst, Blockchain Developer, and FinTech Professional.
chunks.text: Key Roles in High Demand for CSBS Graduates: 1. Business Systems Analyst — Responsibilities: Identify business needs, design systems to meet those needs, ensure technical solutions align with business goals. Skills: Business analysis, Systems design, ERP systems (SAP, Oracle), Agile methodologies, Requirements gathering. 2. Data Scientist / Data Analyst — Responsibilities: Collect, analyze, interpret large datasets to help businesses make informed decisions. Combines business acumen with data analysis and statistical modeling. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning, Data Visualization (Tableau, Power BI), Big Data tools (Hadoop, Spark). 3. Cloud Architect / Cloud Engineer — Responsibilities: Design, implement, manage cloud solutions; help businesses transition to or optimize cloud environments. Skills: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, cloud architecture, Serverless technologies, Cloud security. 4. ERP Consultant / Implementation Specialist — Responsibilities: Implement and customize ERP systems (SAP, Oracle); interact with business stakeholders and technical teams. Skills: ERP software (SAP, Oracle), business process modeling, project management, change management. 5. AI / ML Engineer — Responsibilities: Develop ML algorithms, AI tools and models for demand forecasting, customer service automation, fraud detection. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, AI algorithms, NLP, data preprocessing. 6. Cyber Security Specialist — Responsibilities: Protect business systems from cyber threats, ensure security of sensitive data. Implement firewalls, intrusion detection systems, encryption. Skills: Network security, ethical hacking, penetration testing, data encryption, GDPR compliance. 7. Business Intelligence Analyst — Responsibilities: Use data analytics to help businesses make strategic decisions, generate reports and dashboards. Skills: SQL, Tableau, Power BI, data mining, business analytics, decision support systems. 8. Blockchain Developer — Responsibilities: Design and develop decentralized applications using blockchain (finance, supply chain, business contracts). Skills: Blockchain platforms (Ethereum, Hyperledger), smart contracts, cryptography, distributed ledgers. 9. FinTech Professional — Responsibilities: Design and optimize financial technologies for payments, lending, insurance, investments. Skills: FinTech platforms, blockchain, digital payments, regulatory compliance, data analytics.

chunks.section: Department Overview and Programme Details — B.E. CSE
chunks.context: Overview of the CSE department at MSAJCE and full details of the B.E. Computer Science and Engineering programme including duration, seats, eligibility, and syllabus links.
chunks.text: Department of Computer Science and Engineering — MSAJCE Computer Science and Engineering is one of the popular courses among engineering students. It mainly focuses on programming, computation, analysis of algorithm, program design and testing, software engineering etc for design, development and analysis of software and hardware used to solve the problem of various businesses, scientific and social issues. CSE integrates both computer hardware and software and is intended to develop solid understanding of the entire computer system and its accessories and software. It provides sophistication to the world and touches human lives in all aspects such as education, transportation, entertainment, banking, social media, healthcare and supply chain management. CSE is an interdisciplinary subject linked with psychology, geology science, maths, art, music, bio informatics, electronics, and operation research. B.E. - Computer Science and Engineering Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry) No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry) Intake / No. of Seats: Total - 60 (Government - 30, Management - 30) Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination. Curriculum & Syllabus: 2021 Regulation: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf 2017 Regulation: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2017Regulation.pdf Career Opportunities: Planners, Designers, Constructors & Operators Stewards of the Environment Innovators & Integrators of Technology Managers of risk Leaders in Public Policy Public Sector Undertaking Government Sector Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.
